name: Lazy-Prices stale-filing factor: 10-K/10-Q item-level textual-change (Risk Factors + MD&A) on PIT S&P 500
mechanism: Cohen-Malloy-Nguyen (JF 2020, 'Lazy Prices'): managers leave filing boilerplate untouched unless something changed â€” new risk factors, litigation/going-concern language, hedged MD&A appear BEFORE bad realized outcomes. Investors do not read year-over-year diffs of 100+ page documents, so the information diffuses slowly: high-change filers underperform low-change ('stale') filers over the following 1-2 quarters. The edge is underreaction to costly-to-process information; the feature is pure text with zero price or fundamental-level input, which is why it should be orthogonal to the tapped numeric-factor space. Most of the documented alpha is on the SHORT (changer) side, hence the beta-neutral L-S construction rather than long-only.
why_generalizes: CMN document the effect 1995-2014 across all form types, monotone in change magnitude, strongest precisely in the items we extract (Item 1A Risk Factors, Item 7 MD&A), robust to size/BM/momentum controls. The mechanism (attention scarcity vs document length) is structural, not era-specific. Counter-evidence priced into the prior: published 2020 (arb decay), and large caps â€” our only universe â€” were the WEAKEST segment in the original paper. The test window 2018-2026 is fully post-sample for the paper, so a pass here is genuine OOS confirmation, not replication.
data_sources: SEC EDGAR primary filing documents (10-K, 10-Q) fetched via existing src/market_data/edgar/client.py::fetch_submissions + fetch_filing_text (STOCKNEW_EDGAR_USER_AGENT, ~10 req/s) â€” NEW bounded ingestion ~16-20k docs for PIT S&P 500 2017-2026, cached to data/edgar_cache/filings_text/ (free)
EDGAR submissions JSON recent.acceptanceDateTime (the SEC accept-timestamp) for PIT gating
Frozen snapshots data/snapshots/{acd1e7401c6484cf, a36c9bfd0c353b53, 2c853f10c6638fc0, 57016c1293f136cd, 1c1c314850bb7368, a347da6750f42939, fe045eff04a15142} for prices/SPY/VIX/PIT membership
NO LLM calls â€” mechanical cosine similarity only (deterministic, $0 marginal)
pit_plan: For each rebalance as_of: (1) eligible filings = those with acceptanceDateTime <= as_of (accept-TIMESTAMP from submissions JSON, not filingDate â€” kills same-day lookahead on after-close accepts); (2) current doc = most recent eligible 10-K or 10-Q; comparator = the same-form filing ~1 year prior, also accepted <= as_of; (3) similarity computed item-level (10-K: Item 1A + Item 7/7A; 10-Q: Part II Item 1A + Part I Item 2) so quarterly vs annual docs are never cross-compared; (4) names with no eligible filing in trailing 380d = NaN, excluded, never filled; (5) universe membership from the snapshot's frozen PIT membership (per-rebalance re-resolve, #16 pattern); (6) sidecar filing_delta_signal.json frozen per snapshot keyed by accession number, OUTSIDE the content hash (fundamentals_pit.json / earnings_sidecar.parquet pattern) so run 1 == run N bit-identical. Raw doc cache keyed by accession number is immutable â€” EDGAR filings never mutate, so cache staleness cannot leak.
entry_signal: At each 63d rebalance: score(t) = cosine similarity of TF vectors (1-2 grams, stopword-stripped, HTML-stripped via the existing _strip_html pattern) between the item-level text of the most recent accepted 10-K/10-Q and the same items of the same-form filing one year prior. Higher similarity = staler = bullish. Cross-sectional rank (house convention). Long = top quartile similarity (~24 stalest), short = bottom quartile (~24 biggest changers). Metric, items, and n-gram choice are FIXED now â€” no post-hoc metric shopping (Jaccard/embedding variants are NOT permitted as rescue re-runs).
horizon: 63 trading days (matches house cadence; CMN alpha accrues over ~3-6 months so one-to-two rebalance holds capture it). Signal refreshes per name only when a new filing is accepted (quarterly), so natural turnover is low â€” cost-friendly.
universe: PIT S&P 500 (snapshot-frozen membership with per-rebalance re-resolve), 2018-2026, the 7 breadth-trio rolling windows. No broad-universe extension (killed lever).
construction: Beta-neutral long-short: long ~24 stalest / short ~24 biggest-changers, equal weight within legs, SPY-hedged to beta 0 via the existing run_factor_backtest --long-short path; NO regime gate (--no-regime-filter â€” this is a selection test, gate timing is known-negative ex-COVID); $100M notional; 50bps cost + 50bps borrow. Secondary use ONLY if the gauntlet passes: negative screen (drop bottom-decile changers) on the momval book â€” deferred past the 2026-08-27 forward-paper review; the production composite is frozen and is not touched by this test.
orthogonality: Expected near-zero loading on 12-1 momentum: the feature has no price input. Plausible mild structural correlations: changers cluster in falling/distressed names, so stale-long may be weakly momentum-long and weakly anti-value. Pre-registered orthogonality GATE (Stage 1, before any return is computed): pooled Spearman |rho| vs 12-1 momentum < 0.30 and top-24 overlap with the momentum book < 30% (high_52w_probe pattern, which passed 0.646 and still got killed on tail â€” here the objective is MEAN return so the corr bar is stricter); |rho| vs value < 0.40. Fail = stop, signal is a clone.
preregistered_bar: Fixed BEFORE any return is seen, staged kill-gates: STAGE 0 coverage â€” >=90% of PIT members scored at each rebalance date in every window (else coverage-sparsity kill, the insider failure mode). STAGE 1 orthogonality â€” as in the orthogonality field; fail = stop. STAGE 2 IC â€” factor_lab 63d forward-IC permutation p<0.05 with the SAME sign in >=7/10 cells across the 7 snapshots, AND sign holds in the 2022+ subsample (post-publication decay check); fail = stop, no portfolio run. STAGE 3 portfolio â€” phase-averaged (9 offsets) beta-neutral L-S at 50bps+50bps borrow, $100M: median CAPM Jensen-alpha (OLS) positive in >=6 of 7 rolling windows 2018-2026 AND >=60% of rebalance phases positive (median across windows) AND median alpha >= +3%/yr (below that it cannot survive real-world frictions). Strict WF is NOT a bar (proven edge-magnitude test). NO metric/param re-tuning after Stage 2 results are seen; one shot.
gauntlet_commands: # Stage -1: write the sidecar builder (modeled on scripts/build_filing_signal.py + scripts/build_accruals_sidecar.py; reuses EDGARClient.fetch_submissions/fetch_filing_text + _strip_html); then per window:
# mandatory artifact scrub on every window (guard already in backtester, scan documents it):
# Stage 2: add filing_delta sidecar read to factor_lab base_signals (one-block change, accruals-sidecar pattern at scripts/factor_lab.py:165), then:
# Stage 3: wire filing_delta as a --factor choice in run_factor_backtest (PEAD/--include-pead sidecar pattern), then phase-average the 3 representative regimes:
uv run python scripts/phase_envelope.py --snapshot-id fe045eff04a15142 --base-args "--factor filing_delta --long-short --no-regime-filter --cost-bps 50 --borrow-bps 50 --starting-cash 100000000"
# full 7-window verdict (same args across the breadth trio set):
already_tried_check: Family 'Filing-TEXT signals' is in killed_families â€” this must be addressed head-on. The two killed items were: (1) LLM 8-K TONE PoC (scripts/build_filing_signal.py, gpt-4o-mini, IC ~0/sign-flipping) â€” different filings (8-K vs 10-K/10-Q), different feature (LLM sentiment vs mechanical YoY document diff), different mechanism (tone vs change-blindness underreaction); a tone null does not test the diff signal. (2) 'Universe-wide extraction NOT BUILDABLE' â€” that verdict was about the existing 54-ticker filings_corpus; both ledger notes explicitly list 'universe-wide filing-text ingestion' as the sanctioned NEW-DATA path, and S&P-500-only scopes it to ~16-20k free, rate-limited docs via the existing EDGARClient â€” buildable in hours, no spend. Not a duplicate of catalyst-analyzer (old-system 8-K narrative) or insider-narrative work either. Verdict: genuinely novel test within a scarred family; the scar tissue is reflected in the prior, not hidden.
novelty: First mechanical (non-LLM) filing-diff signal in this codebase; first use of EDGAR accept-timestamps for PIT text; first test targeting the change-blindness mechanism rather than tone/sentiment. Academic provenance (CMN 2020) means it is not novel to the world â€” the novelty claim is OOS-window (2018-2026 post-sample) x our-universe x our-gauntlet.
